subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Explain how historians organise the study of the past
Understand the importance and limitations of dates in history
Evaluate James Mill’s classification of Indian history
Identify and interpret different historical sources
Understand the role of administrative records in writing history

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Study of History
student_activities: Students list events that have changed society
Discuss how past events influence the present

lesson_plan.title: Importance and Limitations of Dates
topics_covered: Limitations of focusing only on dates
student_activities: Create a timeline of key events from Indian history
Discuss why events are remembered more than dates

lesson_plan.title: Periodisation in History
topics_covered: Purpose of dividing history into periods
student_activities: Draw a timeline showing historical periods
Explain why historians divide history this way

lesson_plan.title: James Mill’s View of Indian History
student_activities: Write the three periods suggested by James Mill
Discuss problems with classifying history based on religion

lesson_plan.title: Sources Used by Historians
topics_covered: Types of sources: written, visual and archaeological
Importance of evidence in historical study
student_activities: Discuss how letters, reports and photographs provide historical evidence

lesson_plan.title: Administrative Records and Chapter Recap
topics_covered: Importance of archives and official documents
student_activities: Participate in a group quiz for revision

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse historical ideas rather than memorising dates so that they develop deeper historical thinking.
